title: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE
display_title: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE
wikitext:
[]: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by AsukaShikinami10 » Thu May 27, 2021 10:05 pm Hi everyone, I was recently rewatching the movies and I'm eager to know if anybody has a proper explanation about the three appearences of Quantum Rei in NTE. This time it seems to be different because she's not a God like she became in EoE. So my question is: what is this Rei and why does she appear in the movies at all knowing that Rei II is still trapped in Eva 01. She cannot be the one who appears to Shinji in 1.0 and Rei 06 in 3.0 because she doesn't have those kind of powers this time around. So... Is there any explanation as to who this Quantum Rei is? because for me it doesn't make any sense for her to exist in this continuity since she became a normal human being in the end like Kaworu, not some sort of God with quantum powers. Even in 3.0+1.0 she makes a short appearence but it is never explained if she is supposed to be the same Rei as Rei II or a different one. I tend to think she's not Rei II because when Shinji encounters her inside Eva 01 she's wearing her plugsuit and sporting long hair. Any thoughts?

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri May 28, 2021 2:42 am The long hair is probably a reference to Mari's earlier line when she's cutting Asuka's hair, about how it's proof that she's human. (idk if that's some Japanese cultural thing or a particular fixation of Anno/Tsurumaki/ someone else) As for where the quantum Rei's are "coming from" - either EVA 01 itself, which ascended into a god-like being after all (We saw no 'quantum Yuis' in the og series, but the rules are probably different) or from the golgatha object/Eva Imaginary, which is also GNR-like (perhaps because it's being controlled through the opfer type EVAs with the advanced Ayanami types in them) Of course Gendo mentions that it only looks like Lillith to Shinji & we're never really told what Gendo sees. The actual Lillith also seems to have merged with one out of clones 3-5 before she 'died'. As for why she's a normal human at the end, that probably explicitly because Shinji made it so; We don't see any more floaty Reis after the 'Neon Genesis'

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Konja7 » Fri May 28, 2021 3:55 am It is a mystery where Quantum Rei comes from. In NTE, there seems to still be a connection between Lilith and Rei, so her appearances are likely connected to that. It's possible Rei II could "control" Quantum Rei after the events of Ha (like Rei III in EoE). In Shin, it's possible we even have a Quantum Kaworu. He appears to speak with Shinj (the scene in the trailers), even although Kaworu's soul seems to be trapped in Eva-13 Kendrix wrote: The long hair is probably a reference to Mari's earlier line when she's cutting Asuka's hair, about how it's proof that she's human. (idk if that's some Japanese cultural thing or a particular fixation of Anno/Tsurumaki/ someone else)

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri May 28, 2021 4:27 am Konja7 wrote: It's possible Rei II could "control" Quantum Rei after the events of Ha (like Rei III in EoE).

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri May 28, 2021 4:54 am Wait, Quantum Rei also appears in Shin? When does that happens exactly?

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri May 28, 2021 9:04 am ElMariachi wrote: Wait, Quantum Rei also appears in Shin? When does that happens exactly?

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri May 28, 2021 2:50 pm Kendrix wrote: There's at least 1 confirmed sighting (even in one of the trailers) while Shinji is at the ruins.

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat May 29, 2021 8:48 am ElMariachi wrote: You know, there's something funny: both Rei and Asuka have their hair seen growing, Kaworu's hair length and style change between Ha and Q, which is supposed to symbolize that deep down they are human, and the only ones who's hair style and length are never seen changing... are Shinji and Mari, I wonder if that's supposed to mean something?

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat May 29, 2021 10:24 am Kendrix wrote: Maybe in Shinji's case, it's because he didn't experience anything those 14 years. That, or the WILLE ppl chopped it off. (or, most likely, Anno & co had not had that idea yet)

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat May 29, 2021 10:34 am Kendrix wrote: Maybe in Shinji's case, it's because he didn't experience anything those 14 years. That, or the WILLE ppl chopped it off. (or, most likely, Anno & co had not had that idea yet)

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat May 29, 2021 10:44 am ElMariachi wrote: But Rei II didn't experienced anything either yet she got the "Reipunzel" treatment.

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat May 29, 2021 11:13 am She seems to at least vaguely recall the Suzuhara baby - the doll she's holding is based on her; Though it's raggedy look and the 'warehouse' surroundings very much give me a 'lonely kid holding comfort toy' vibe. I don't think she's supposed to look 'older' tho, with Asuka make it very obvious, drawing their lips fuller and making Big Asuka burst out of her plugsuit, with the chair and the jacket for comparison, and in the epilogue Big Mari also gets more pronounced lips. Maybe when she comes back she'll have all the experiences from all the Reis combined. (which isn't much; Besides the two we see,most were either only active for a short time or spent most their time on standby in the opfer type EVAs, little that might be considered as gaining life experience. The most salient might be the few months between NTI and ATI, in which there was Sweater Vest Rei. ) * Rei shows up in the village * "Hi class rep." * Hikari reacts in surprise at the recognition * "Thanks for looking after me when I was all confused, in a way you helped have the normal childhood I never got. I very much want to stay here just like you offered." * Rei lives with the Suzuharas for the rest of her adolescence, Tsubame calls her Onee-chan once she masters talking * * also, she keeps a kitten * Though I'm not really settled on that interpretation; I've always seen them as more separate. Though in the end they're neither completely the same nor completely separate; It's genuinely complicated.

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat May 29, 2021 11:32 am ElMariachi wrote: Which makes me think, in the end we never learned how/why was Shinji recreated by Unit 01 and not Rei, whether it happened on its own or if it was WILLE who did it, but didn't tried to do it for Rei because they didn't know she was inside. (which raises the question of why would they thought that, since they saw Zeruel's core turning into a giant Rei and be absorbed by Unit 01)

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat May 29, 2021 11:53 am The reports have been kinda inconclusive as to wether she's physically in the seat or 'ghostly' (she looks solid in the 'warehouse' sequence but that's in instrumentality), & Gendo has a cryptic line addressing her that is unclear without the visual - Was there a plan of some sort? Was she even acting consciously, or was it an effect of her last thoughts before she was eaten (we're shown that before she attacks Zeruel, she was thinking that she wanted Shinji to get away; indeed she even tells Asuka to run in the elevator scene but given recent revelations, it's unclear if Asuka even could have. Maybe the reason she got so mad is that she, too, is "bound to it", but she didn't want to think of herself as trapped or a victim. Or it could be Rei telling her to escape when she can't even if she wanted to. ) Could Rei have left earlier? If not, why? Something to do with the Final Impact ritual, or a calculation that Shinji might be killed if he can still sync? (that would require her to be aware of the outside tho - though the quantum Reis might imply that she has some idea of what's going on) If she stayed intentionally you might consider it a parallel to Yui (or perhaps more of a 'not so different' moment, given their opposite intentions) One wonders what went on there; was there a struggle, or did Yui just lean back & assume that everything would go according to plan no matter what anyone does? It is a different decision if she could've left but didn't (cause she would be giving up on life to spare Shinji some unpleasantness ) vs. if she couldn't/ counted herself lost but still chose to do what she could to help (like Hotaru in Sailor Moon Crystal, or indeed, Kaworu) Though another thing to consider is that if she left, she would be dead in a few months and Gendo would get to reuse the soul fragment. She might prefer staying 'frozen' to the loss of identity, or lack the faith that the short life she could have would be worth it (until ReiQ proved the opposite) Ritsuko said they 'found' Shinji, so they might not have retrieved him on purpose or even thought that possible. Maybe Rei let him out as soon as she could safely do so (ie, when they were no longer speared, in mid third impact or in space. )

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun May 30, 2021 11:20 am About Rei's level of consciousness inside EVA-01, maybe like Kendrix suggests, she was operating on a semi-conscious level, like in a sort of dream state, influenced by her thought at the moment she was absorbed (in this case, preventing Shinji from piloting EVA-01), the same way that Yui seem to only manifest herself when her son is in danger or it's not him piloting her. Similarly, it's heavily implied that it was Shinji who subconsciously briefly awoke EVA-01 to save Asuka at the beginning of Q, yet he doesn't have any recollection of it once extracted from the Eva. And while Rei is in a dream state while Unit 01 is dormant and only used to power the Wunder, she completely awakes when the Eva is activated, maybe thanks to a link with Shinji born from both of them having been together inside EVA-01 for 14 years and/or because the circumstances of their absorption was different than Yui's. Or it could also be that like in NGE, Rei has Lilith's soul and Unit 01 was made from Lilith and that it explains why she was awake when Shinji activated the Eva at the end of Shin. Kendrix wrote: If she stayed intentionally you might consider it a parallel to Yui (or perhaps more of a 'not so different' moment, given their opposite intentions) One wonders what went on there; was there a struggle, or did Yui just lean back & assume that everything would go according to plan no matter what anyone does?

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sun May 30, 2021 12:11 pm I think Rei is totally conscious when she makes Shinji's synchronicity to look like 0, because she seems to know everything about this situation (including that Shinji's real synchronicity is Infinity). Also, it would fit Rei's theme, she couldn't left the Eva since she wouldn't survive outside. ElMariachi wrote: We don't actually know if WILLE would had been unable to do anything to save Rei Q had the Wunder arrived slightly earlier. (really, it's quite the plot contrivance that they arrived literally the day after her death, it would had been interesting to see how they react to an Ayanami type and if they would had tried to do something to save her)

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun May 30, 2021 12:28 pm Konja7 wrote: I think Rei is totally conscious when she makes Shinji's synchronicity to look like 0, because she seems to know everything about this situation (including that Shinji's real synchronicity is Infinity).

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun May 30, 2021 3:39 pm ElMariachi wrote: From what I understood from the script, the 0% synch ratio was a displaying error from the measuring instruments, because they don't have a setting to calculate an infinite ratio.

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sun May 30, 2021 4:31 pm ElMariachi wrote: From what I understood from the script, the 0% synch ratio was a displaying error from the measuring instruments, because they don't have a setting to calculate an infinite ratio.

[]: Re: Explanation of Quantum Rei in NTE             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jun 01, 2021 4:10 am Konja7 wrote: I think I was confused by the first words of Rei II. She apologize to Shinji because she wasn't able to keep him from piloting the Eva. That said, in the dialogue, it seems that Shinji calls Rei before getting into Eva-01. So, Rei may need to allow Shinji to get into Eva-01 and pilot it. If we saw the scene, we could understand it better.
